Hi, I do not think we have met. I am Mark, a cloud architect from Toronto.
Hi Mark, I am Daniela, an IT manager from Sao Paulo. By the way, how was your trip?
Long, but worth it. I mostly work on cloud security for banks.
Oh, really? That is interesting. How did you get into cloud security?
Honestly, by accident. My first job was support, and I just loved solving problems.
We have something in common then. I started in support too. What do you enjoy most about it?
The people, really. You meet someone new at every event. Speaking of which, we should stay in touch.
I would love that. Here is my card, feel free to reach out. I look forward to staying in touch.

It was great to meet you. Feel free to reach out, I look forward to staying in touch.

Good evening, everyone, and welcome to the International Tech Leaders mixer. We are so glad you could join us tonight. Before the conversations begin, here is a quick tip from the host team. The goal of tonight is not to collect as many business cards as possible. The goal is to have a few real conversations. So when you meet someone new, do not stop after the first question. Ask a follow-up question. If someone tells you what they do, ask how they got into it, or what they enjoy most about it. Show that you are really listening. A little small talk about the city, the food, or the trip here is a great way to start. And if you find something in common, talk about it. People remember the person who was curious about them, not the person who only talked about themselves. The bar is open, the food is ready, and there are over two hundred professionals here tonight who would love to meet you. So take a deep breath, walk up to someone, and keep the conversation going. Enjoy your evening.

Hi Daniela, it is Paulo here, just a quick voice note before your event tonight. I know networking in English can feel stressful, so let me give you three simple ideas. First, you do not need perfect English. You need good questions. When someone answers, react and ask one more thing. Just say, oh really, tell me more about that, and they will keep talking. Second, look for something in common. Maybe you both work in technology, maybe you both traveled a long way to be there. When you find it, the conversation gets easy. Third, do not be afraid to bring up a new topic. By the way, how was your trip, is a perfectly good way to keep things going. And at the end, do not just walk away. Wrap it up nicely. Say it was great to meet you, and that you look forward to staying in touch. You are going to do great tonight. Go and enjoy it, and send me a message tomorrow to tell me how it went.
